breadth.note: 5 of 11 sectors now Leading on 1m RS — XLF +5.89, XLV +5.42, XLU +3.62, XLI +3.45 AND XLRE flipping back to Leading (−0.31→+0.04). The DECISIVE morning delta vs the pre-market thesis: the tech RS heal STALLED and REVERSED — XLK 1m RS −0.53→−0.94 (3m +20.25→+19.74), rolling back AWAY from the 0-cross it was approaching, so the 'handoff back to tech' did NOT confirm at the open. Instead FINANCIALS accelerated hard (XLF +4.86→+5.89, the strongest 1m RS on the board) and the cyclical/rate-sensitive/defensive block (XLV, XLI, XLU) held Leading. The lagging tail persists: XLE −6.05→−6.37 (still the worst, deteriorating), XLC −3.25 (worst RRG-Lagging). XLY (−1.06) and XLP (−0.29) improved but stayed Weakening. Proxy from sector-member bars, not an exchange breadth feed.

regime.note: MORNING = the overnight gap HELD but the pre-market 'leadership handoff back to tech' thesis did NOT get intraday confirmation — it PARTLY reversed. The complex is still up vs the 7/8 close (QQQ +1.10%, SPMO +2.16%, VGT +1.69%) but gave a sliver back off the pre-market highs, and — the key tell — the momentum HEAL faded intraday: SPMO's 5-day slipped back negative (−0.27→−0.97), QQQ's eased (−0.58→−0.81), and VGT's cross above 0 shrank to a whisker (+0.31→+0.17). Under the hood the rotation went the OTHER way from the pre-market call: XLK 1m RS rolled back from the 0-cross (−0.53→−0.94) while FINANCIALS accelerated to the top of the board (XLF +5.89) and the cyclical/defensive/rate-sensitive leadership (XLV/XLI/XLU/XLRE) broadened to 5 Leading sectors. So the actionable read INVERTS the pre-market lean: the tape is holding risk-on, but leadership is NOT tech — it is financials + cyclicals + defensives, and the tech re-entry trigger (XLK 1m RS >0) FAILED to fire and pulled back. One offsetting positive: MSFT mean-reverted UP +0.62% off its low (5d −2.02→−1.41), narrowing yesterday's mega-cap divergence. VIX kept falling (16.57) = the gap is being held orderly, positive-gamma. Falsifiers of THIS morning read: XLK 1m RS re-crossing >0 (tech re-asserts and the pre-market handoff resumes); the complex's 5-day momentum re-flipping positive; a 30y auction TAIL at 1pm re-pressuring duration; or a full gap-fill back through the 7/8 close (QQQ ~711) turning it into an exhaustion gap. The read leans 'gap held, leadership = financials/cyclicals, tech re-entry deferred until XLK RS reclaims 0' — with the 1pm auction the live intraday risk into CPI (7/14).

attention.title: Tech RS heal STALLED at the open — XLK 1m RS rolled BACK from the 0-cross (−0.53→−0.94); the pre-market handoff-to-tech did NOT confirm
attention.what: The pre-market thesis was 'XLK RS healing a 4th read toward a positive cross = handoff back to tech.' The morning slice REVERSED it: XLK 1m RS −0.53→−0.94 (3m +20.25→+19.74), pulling AWAY from 0. Meanwhile FINANCIALS accelerated to the top of the board (XLF +4.86→+5.89) and the cyclical/defensive/rate-sensitive block held Leading (XLV +5.42, XLI +3.45, XLU +3.62), with XLRE flipping back to Leading (+0.04). Est. from Tier-A sector bars, 11:00 ET.
attention.why: This is the run's most actionable delta and it INVERTS the pre-market call: the tape is holding risk-on, but leadership is NOT tech — it is financials + cyclicals + defensives. The tech re-entry trigger prior runs set (XLK 1m RS crossing >0) FAILED to fire and reversed, so a tech-momentum add is now DEFERRED, not confirmed. Trade the actual leader (XLF, accelerating) and wait for XLK 1m RS to reclaim >0 before re-adding tech beta. Negate of this read: XLK 1m RS re-crosses >0 and the complex's 5-day re-flips positive.

attention.title: Gap HELD but the 5-day-cross trigger FADED intraday — SPMO/QQQ 5-day slipped back negative, VGT's cross shrank to a whisker
attention.what: The complex held most of the overnight gap (QQQ +1.10%, SPMO +2.16%, VGT +1.69% vs the 7/8 close) but gave a sliver back off the pre-market print (QQQ 720.95→719.30, SPMO 154.90→153.82, VGT 117.58→117.42) AND the 5-day momentum heal reversed: SPMO −0.27→−0.97, QQQ −0.58→−0.81, VGT +0.31→+0.17 (barely positive). 21/63-day trend still intact (SPMO +4.2/+26.9, QQQ +0.6/+18.8). Est. from watchlist bars.
attention.why: The pre-market 'add on the 5-day cross' trigger is NO LONGER cleanly hitting — only VGT is fractionally above 0 and shrinking, SPMO/QQQ slipped back under. That downgrades the momentum add from 'confirmation arriving' to 'confirmation faded — wait.' The trend structure (21/63d) is unbroken so this is a stall, not a break; the level to hold is the 7/8 close / gap base (QQQ ~711). A clean re-flip of the 5-day back >0 with XLK RS reclaiming 0 is what re-arms the add.

attention.title: Financials are the CLEAR morning leader — XLF 1m RS ACCELERATED to +5.89 (top of the board) into the 7/14 big-bank kickoff

attention.why: With the tech-RS handoff stalling, financials are the cleanest actionable rotation long: accelerating RS, Leading RRG, and a bank-earnings catalyst 3 sessions out. A holding/steepening curve after today's 1pm 30y auction + solid bank prints is the presser. Add/hold; press-trigger is XLF 1m RS holding >5 through the first bank reports; negate is a break back under +3 (curve bull-flattens / yields collapse).

The morning answer so far: it is mean-reverting UP toward the group (healthy), not dragging the complex lower. But it is still the weakest name (21d −8.0) and a two-sided EVENT trade into the 7/29 FOMC-day print — a stabilizing tell for the complex, not a momentum long. Watch whether it can reclaim its 5-day back positive.

recommendations.subject: Tech-momentum add (SPMO / VGT / QQQ): DOWNGRADE from 'add' to WAIT — the 5-day cross faded and XLK RS pulled back at the open

Re-arm the long only when XLK 1m RS reclaims >0 AND the complex's 5-day re-flips positive, holding the 7/8 gap base (QQQ ~711). Full gap-fill through ~711 red is the negate (exhaustion gap). Estimate, not advice.

recommendations.subject: Stay with the CONFIRMED leadership — financials + cyclicals + defensives (XLF/XLV/XLI/XLU/XLRE), NOT tech, until XLK RS reclaims 0
recommendations.rationale: The morning slice broadened the non-tech leadership to 5 Leading sectors (XLF +5.89, XLV +5.42, XLU +3.62, XLI +3.45, XLRE flipping back to +0.04) while the tech re-entry trigger FAILED (XLK 1m RS −0.53→−0.94). The actionable rotation is to favor the confirmed leaders — cyclicals (financials/industrials), defensives (healthcare) and rate-sensitives (utilities/REITs) — over tech beta for now. Trigger to rotate BACK toward tech: XLK 1m RS crossing >0 confirmed by the complex's 5-day re-flipping positive. Negate (stay non-tech) as long as XLK RS stays sub-zero. Estimate from RRG + momentum-Δ, not advice.

events.psychologyNote: Estimate — symmetric prior (30/40/30) as no fresh macro signal changed the read; both a hot and a cold print carry a tech tail via different channels. Far-out vs the 7/9 claims + CPI/PPI cluster — listed last.

psychology.read: REGIME: a risk-on gap that HELD but whose intraday leadership DIVERGED from the pre-market thesis — an orderly, positive-gamma tape where the momentum complex kept most of the overnight gap (QQQ +1.10%, SPMO +2.16%, VGT +1.69% vs the 7/8 close) with VIX ticking DOWN again to 16.57, but the 'handoff back to tech' did NOT confirm at the open. NAME it: a calm, dealer-dampened risk-on regime with FINANCIALS/CYCLICALS/DEFENSIVES leadership, NOT a tech re-acceleration. The decisive morning tell: XLK 1m RS rolled BACK from the 0-cross it was approaching (−0.53→−0.94) and the complex's 5-day momentum heal reversed (SPMO −0.27→−0.97, QQQ −0.58→−0.81, VGT's cross shrank to +0.17) — so the pre-market 'add on the 5-day cross' trigger FADED. CROWD POSTURE: the defensive-hedge unwind PAUSED and leadership CONSOLIDATED in financials — XLF accelerated to the top of the board (+4.86→+5.89) and the Leading cohort broadened to 5 sectors (XLF/XLV/XLI/XLU/XLRE) while tech beta stalled. Complacency is intact (VIX 16.57, still falling) and the tape is holding risk-on, but the crowd is expressing it through cyclicals/financials, not the mega-cap growth that led the gap. One stabilizer cuts the other way: MSFT mean-reverted UP +0.62% off its low (5d −2.02→−1.41), NARROWING yesterday's mega-cap divergence rather than spreading it. FALSIFIERS of THIS read: (1) XLK 1m RS re-crossing >0 and the complex's 5-day re-flipping positive (tech re-asserts, the pre-market handoff resumes); (2) a 30y auction TAIL at 1pm re-pressuring duration and breaking the held gap; (3) a full gap-fill back through the 7/8 close (QQQ ~711) turning it into an exhaustion gap; (4) VIX term structure inverting to BACKWARDATION (IVTS >1.0). The read leans 'gap held, leadership = financials/cyclicals/defensives, tech re-entry DEFERRED until XLK RS reclaims 0' — with the 1pm auction the live intraday risk into CPI (7/14).
inputs: GAP HELD but eased off the pre-market print: QQQ 720.95→719.30 (−0.23%), SPMO 154.90→153.82 (−0.70%), VGT 117.58→117.42 (−0.14%) — still +1.1-2.2% vs the 7/8 AH close

5-day momentum heal REVERSED intraday: SPMO −0.27→−0.97, QQQ −0.58→−0.81, VGT's cross shrank to +0.17 — the 'add on the 5-day cross' trigger faded; 21/63d trend intact (SPMO +4.2/+26.9, QQQ +0.6/+18.8)
TECH RS STALLED: XLK 1m RS rolled BACK from the 0-cross (−0.53→−0.94), 3m RS +20.25→+19.74 — the handoff-to-tech did NOT confirm
FINANCIALS accelerated to the top of the board: XLF 1m RS +4.86→+5.89 (strongest, Leading) — the one sector accelerating in both slices
Leading cohort broadened to 5 sectors: XLF +5.89, XLV +5.42, XLU +3.62, XLI +3.45, XLRE flipping back to +0.04
MSFT MEAN-REVERTED UP 376.53→378.88 (+0.62% off its low), 5d recovering −2.02→−1.41 — yesterday's mega-cap divergence NARROWING, not spreading
VIX ticked DOWN again to 16.57 (−0.17) as the gap held — orderly / positive-gamma, no vol re-expansion
VIX term structure carried CONTANGO (IVTS ~0.85, VIX3M ~19; re-pull VIX9D/VIX3M live) = orderly; falsifier is IVTS >1.0
ENERGY still the WORST and deteriorating: XLE 1m RS −6.05→−6.37 — a crude-fade proxy that keeps easing the headline-CPI tail; re-verify WTI live
claims PRINTED 8:30am (pre-open) — result NOT in the slice, but the tape held the gap through it (inferred, not fetched); 30y auction $22B reopening at 1pm is the next live catalyst
10y stopped 4.58% at the 7/8 auction (2.6 bid-to-cover, soft/average) — the reference into today's 30y
Fear&Greed NOT fetched this run — sentiment band unknown, not assumed
psychology.tiltOnProbabilities: The morning slice INVERTS the pre-market rotation lean: tech-RS stalled (XLK −0.94) and the 5-day cross faded, so the momentum add is DOWNGRADED to WATCH and financials (XLF +5.89) become the confirmed long. TODAY's read shifted from 'two prints ahead' to 'claims behind, 30y auction (1pm) ahead' — solid-auction base case held at 0.55 (tape voted risk-on pre-auction), the tail nudged to 0.30 as the cleaner negater of the held gap given the morning's tech rollover. CPI (7/14) odds kept symmetric (28/44/28): the fading oil tail (XLE −6.37) still removes the headline upward bias, but a cool print now meets a market whose tech leg has stalled — so the 'squeeze fuel' is more about re-arming than extending. OPEX pin held 0.55 (VIX low/falling). MSFT miss shaded slightly heavier than the STALE option-implied for sell-the-news asymmetry, though its morning mean-reversion up tempers that. All labeled estimates seeded from STALE options (Jul-6) / unverified consensus / carried crude — re-seed live.

QQQ.status: $719.30 (HELD the gap, +1.10% vs the 7/8 close 711.44, but −0.23% off the pre-market print); 5d −0.81 (slipped back from −0.58 — the heal reversed intraday), 21d +0.56, 63d +18.8. The gap is holding risk-on but the 5-day cross faded. The add is DEFERRED: re-arm only when the 5-day re-flips >0 AND XLK 1m RS (−0.94, rolled back) reclaims 0, holding the gap base (~711). A full fade through ~711 red is the exhaustion-gap negate.

SPMO.status: $153.82 (HELD +2.16% vs 150.57 but −0.70% off the pre-market 154.90); 5d slipped back to −0.97 (from −0.27 — the biggest intraday give-back in the book), 21d +4.17 (rising), 63d +26.94 (strongest trend in the watchlist). The purest momentum/semis long — led the overnight resolution-up but the 5-day cross it was approaching faded this morning. Trend (21/63d) intact so it's a stall, not a break; the 5-day re-flip >0 is the re-arm. Carries the most semis beta into any CPI surprise.

VGT.status: $117.42 (HELD +1.69% vs 115.47, −0.14% off pre-market); 5d +0.17 (still positive but shrank from +0.31), 21d +0.26, 63d +27.25. Highest-beta tech proxy sitting in XLK (Weakening, RS rolled BACK to −0.94). Its 5-day is the only one still >0 but barely — the leading tech tell is fading, not confirming. Leads the complex on any tech re-acceleration; falls hardest on a hot-CPI de-gross or a full gap-fade.

MSFT.status: $378.88 (MEAN-REVERTED UP +0.62% off its pre-market low 376.53, still −1.16% vs the 7/8 close); 5d recovering −2.02→−1.41, 21d −7.98, 63d +1.43. The lone laggard is closing part of yesterday's mega-cap divergence rather than dragging the complex down — a stabilizing tell. Still the weakest name; a two-sided EVENT trade into the Jul-29 print, which lands the SAME day as the FOMC decision (double catalyst) — not a momentum long. Implied move ~6% STALE (Jul-6).

experimental.title: Leadership-rotation reversal detector: defensive-RS decay + lagging-group-RS recovery crossover (idea)
experimental.note: Method: track the SPREAD between the average 1m RS of the current Leading defensives/financials (XLV/XLP/XLU/XLF) and the deepest Lagging growth group (XLK) run-over-run; flag a 'rotation reversal' when the spread COMPRESSES for N consecutive reads WHILE the lagging group's RS climbs toward a 0 cross — and flag a FAILED head-fake when that heal REVERSES (this run is a live example: pre-market XLK RS healed a 4th read to −0.53 approaching the cross, then the morning slice ROLLED IT BACK to −0.94 while XLF ACCELERATED to +5.89 — the handoff-to-tech was a head-fake and leadership stayed with financials). Base-rate the forward outcome: does the crossover precede a durable leadership handoff or a failed head-fake like today's? Inputs: sector daily bars + the RRG-lite RS-momentum already in the Tier-A slice. No owning tool watches the two-sided crossover + its failure today; it drove this run's 'tech re-entry deferred, favor financials' read. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Overnight-gap continuation-vs-fade base rate: does a low-VIX trend gap hold or fill? (idea)
experimental.note: Method: tag pre-market sessions where the momentum complex gaps ≥+1% overnight WHILE VIX ticks DOWN (this run: QQQ +1.34%, SPMO +2.88%, VGT +1.83% pre-market, VIX 16.9→16.74 — a positive-gamma trend-continuation gap). Then measure the open→late-morning→close path: this run the gap HELD but eased (QQQ 720.95→719.30 by 11:00) with the intraday leadership rotating AWAY from the gap leaders (tech RS stalled, financials led) — a 'held-but-narrowed' outcome distinct from a clean hold or a full fill. What fraction hold the gap base vs partially narrow vs fully fill, conditioned on the SPY dealer-gamma sign (spot above flip = pin/hold bias)? Build the base rate that turns 'the gap invites a fade' from folklore into a probability, with the gap origin (prior close, QQQ ~711) and the 50-day as the hold/fail lines. Inputs: watchlist + index pre-market/intraday vs prior-close bars (have), ^VIX at each window (have), SPY gamma flip from the gamma lab. Directly frames this run's key open-risk. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Mega-cap breadth-divergence detector: complex up, largest weight down (idea)
experimental.note: Method: flag when the momentum/tech complex advances (QQQ/VGT/SPMO green) WHILE the single largest constituent diverges the other way (this run: MSFT −1.78% and 5-day rolling negative while QQQ/VGT/SPMO gapped up). Quantify the divergence (name return − complex return, plus the name's RS vs the group) and base-rate the forward outcome: does the laggard mean-revert UP to the group (healthy broadening) or does the group roll over TO the laggard (a topping tell, since the biggest weight leads the cap-weighted index)? Inputs: watchlist member bars + index-weight table (MSFT ~4.7% SPX / ~9.9% QQQ). No owning tool watches the largest-weight-vs-complex divergence today; it is this run's main caveat to the gap-up handoff. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: VIX term-structure regime flag: contango→backwardation cross (idea)
experimental.note: Method: track IVTS = VIX/VIX3M (and VIX9D/VIX) daily; flag the cross of IVTS through 1.0 (contango→backwardation) as a regime-change trigger, with day-count of the current regime and a base-rate study of forward SPX returns/realized-vol after each cross. This run's whole read hinges on the curve STAYING in contango (IVTS ~0.85 carried); backwardation is the named falsifier. Inputs: ^VIX/^VIX9D/^VIX3M series (add VIX9D/3M to rlData). No owning tool watches the curve cross today. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Rate-driven rotation gauge: XLK-vs-XLF RS overlaid on the 10y yield (idea)
experimental.note: Method: plot XLK/XLF (and XLK/XLV) relative-strength ratio against the 10y Treasury yield and its 5/20-day change; regress sector RS-momentum on d(10y) to quantify how rate-sensitive the current leadership rotation is, and flag when the correlation breaks (rotation losing its rate driver = tech re-entry — which XLK's 4-read RS heal this run signals). Inputs: XLK/XLF/XLV daily bars (have), ^TNX or 10y yield series (need to add to rlData), with TODAY's 7/9 30y auction as a live test. This run's thesis is explicitly rate+oil-driven — no owning tool ties sector RS to yields today. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Crude/geopolitical shock → energy RRG divergence monitor (idea)
experimental.note: Method: flag when a sector's trailing RRG cell diverges sharply from its recent driver — either a top daily performer stuck deeply Lagging, OR (this run) a sector collapsing on its 1m RS (XLE −4.2→−6.05, the worst mover) as its carried commodity driver fades — a trend-vs-event divergence. Overlay WTI level + its 5/20-day change on XLE relative-strength; a rolling-over XLE is a live PROXY that crude is fading (which eases the headline-CPI tail), and 'trend-follow long' only triggers when crude HOLDS a level AND 1m RS crosses back >0. Inputs: XLE + sector daily bars (have), CL/WTI series (need to add). Recurs whenever an oil move (up or down) decouples from the lagging energy tape and doubles as the CPI-tail read. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Double-catalyst vol event-study: FOMC-day + mega-cap earnings collision (idea)
experimental.note: Method: event-study base rates for days when an FOMC decision and a top-10 S&P name's earnings land together (Jul 29 = FOMC + MSFT) — measure realized-vs-implied vol, average |move|, and next-day drift vs single-catalyst days. Inputs: historical FOMC dates + mega-cap earnings dates + SPX/QQQ realized vol + straddle-implied. Quantifies the compounding this run flags qualitatively. Not yet built; promote if the pattern recurs across quarters.
